questions.prompt: How is the future tense formed in Croatian?
questions.explanation: The future tense uses short forms of htjeti (ću, ćeš, će, ćemo, ćete, će) + infinitive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct future tense: 'Sutra ___ ići na more.'
questions.explanation: Ću = I will (1st person singular future auxiliary).
Hoćeš li = will you (question form). Doći = to come.
Vrijeme = weather (also means 'time'). Klima = climate. Kiša = rain.

questions.prompt: 'Mi ___ putovati sljedeći mjesec.' — Correct future form?
questions.explanation: Ćemo = we will (1st person plural future auxiliary).
Proljeće = spring. Ljeto = summer. Jesen = autumn. Zima = winter.

questions.correctAnswer: I won't / I don't want to
questions.explanation: Neću = ne + ću. It means both 'I won't' and 'I don't want to' depending on context.
Ću = I will. Sutra ću učiti = Tomorrow I will study.

questions.prompt: Oni ___ doći u subotu. (will, 3rd person plural)
questions.explanation: Će is used for both 3rd person singular AND plural in the future tense.
Sunčano = sunny. Used impersonally: 'Danas je sunčano' = It's sunny today.

questions.prompt: ___ li doći na večeru? (Will you, informal)
questions.explanation: Hoćeš li = Will you (informal question). Full form of 'ćeš' used in questions.
Pada kiša = It's raining. Literally 'Rain is falling.'

questions.prompt: Mi ___ vas posjetiti sljedeći tjedan. (will, 1st plural)
questions.explanation: Ćemo = we will. Mi ćemo posjetiti = We will visit.

questions.correctAnswer: Tomorrow we will go to the beach.
acceptableAnswers: We will go to the beach tomorrow.
Tomorrow we'll go to the beach.
questions.explanation: Sutra = tomorrow. Ćemo ići = we will go. Na plažu = to the beach (accusative).

questions.correctAnswer: What is the weather like today?
questions.explanation: Kakvo = what kind. Vrijeme = weather. Danas = today.

acceptableAnswers: It will not rain this weekend.
questions.explanation: Neće = won't (ne + će). Padati kiša = to rain. Ovaj vikend = this weekend.

questions.prompt: Ljeti je vrlo vruće u Dalmaciji.
questions.correctAnswer: In summer it is very hot in Dalmatia.
acceptableAnswers: It's very hot in Dalmatia in summer.
In the summer it's very hot in Dalmatia.
questions.explanation: Ljeti = in summer. Vrlo = very. Vruće = hot.

questions.correctAnswer: What will you do for Christmas?
acceptableAnswers: What are you going to do for Christmas?
questions.explanation: Ćeš raditi = you will do. Za Božić = for Christmas.

questions.hint: When the infinitive ends in -ti and is followed by ću/ćeš, the -i drops: nazvati → nazvat ću.
questions.explanation: Nazvat ću = I will call (infinitive -i drops before ću). Te = you (acc).
Će biti = will be. Hladno = cold. Weather uses impersonal constructions.

questions.prompt: Will you come to the party? (formal)
questions.explanation: Hoćete li = will you (formal question). Doći = to come. Na zabavu = to the party.
Pada snijeg = it's snowing (literally: snow is falling). Same pattern as 'pada kiša'.
Nećemo = we won't (ne + ćemo). Sljedeći tjedan = next week.

questions.prompt: Give the future tense auxiliary forms of 'htjeti' (short forms).
questions.correctAnswer: ja ću, ti ćeš, on/ona/ono će, mi ćemo, vi ćete, oni/one/ona će
questions.hint: The 3rd person singular and plural are the same: 'će'.
questions.explanation: The future auxiliary: ću, ćeš, će, ćemo, ćete, će. Add infinitive after: ću raditi = I will work.
questions.exampleSentence: Ću ti pomoći s domaćom zadaćom.
questions.exampleTranslation: I will help you with homework.

questions.prompt: Give the negative future forms (ne + htjeti).
questions.correctAnswer: ja neću, ti nećeš, on/ona/ono neće, mi nećemo, vi nećete, oni/one/ona neće
questions.hint: The negative forms merge 'ne' with the auxiliary.
questions.explanation: Ne + ću = neću, ne + ćeš = nećeš, etc. Written as one word.
questions.exampleSentence: Nećemo ići van ako pada kiša.
questions.exampleTranslation: We won't go out if it rains.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'htjeti' (to want) in the present tense — full forms.
questions.correctAnswer: ja hoću, ti hoćeš, on/ona/ono hoće, mi hoćemo, vi hoćete, oni/one/ona hoće
questions.hint: Full forms are used in questions: 'Hoćeš li...?'
questions.explanation: Htjeti has both short (ću) and long (hoću) present forms. Long forms are used in questions and emphasis.
questions.exampleTranslation: Would you like a glass of water?

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'doći' (to come) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja dođem, ti dođeš, on/ona/ono dođe, mi dođemo, vi dođete, oni/one/ona dođu
questions.hint: Irregular — based on 'ići' but with the prefix 'do-' and stem change.
questions.explanation: Doći → dođem. The đ appears in all present tense forms. Same pattern as other prefixed forms of ići.
questions.exampleTranslation: When you come home, call me.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'vidjeti' (to see) in the present tense.
questions.correctAnswer: ja vidim, ti vidiš, on/ona/ono vidi, mi vidimo, vi vidite, oni/one/ona vide
questions.explanation: Vidjeti → vidim. Same pattern as voljeti and živjeti.
